description: boofuzz is a fork of and successor to the famous Sulley fuzzing framework. Generates and sends malformed inputs to network services.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/boofuzz && python3 boofuzz.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/boofuzz && python3 boofuzz.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/boofuzz && python3 boofuzz.py --help`. Network protocol fuzzer.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: Network protocol fuzzer.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Network protocol fuzzer.

name: boofuzz

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
